items.md: Between 1945 and 1957, the value of overseas exports doubled. Direct investment in foreign countries rose from $7.2 billion in 1946 to $49.3 billion in 1965, representing 60 percent of the world's total. The largest and most powerful U.S. corporations owned this investment (Jezer, *Dark Ages*, 75).
